title: Truth in Game Design
creator: Jonathan Blow
description: A GDC Europe lecture arguing that designers can study the consequences of a coherent rule system instead of forcing every result to conform to a predetermined message or scenario.
useful: This is a central statement of Blow's design philosophy and gives readers a precise argument they can compare with the mechanics and structure of his games.

manualLinkStatus.note: GDC Vault session page opened successfully during editorial review.

audience: Designers and critics interested in systemic meaning, emergence, and the relationship between authored intention and discoveries made during development.
learning: Distinguish investigating a system from imposing a conclusion
Explain how simple rules can produce unplanned consequences
Evaluate the claim that games can model discoverable truths

editorial.note: Verified against the GDC Vault session record.
